claim: V-9302 causes antitumor responses by increasing cell death and oxidative stress.

result.claim: V-9302 causes antitumor responses by increasing cell death and oxidative stress.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper ID 5256564, titled "Pharmacological Blockade of ASCT2-dependent Glutamine Transport Leads To Anti-tumor Efficacy in Preclinical Models". The abstract of this paper explicitly states that "Pharmacological blockade of ASCT2 with V-9302 resulted in attenuated cancer cell growth and proliferation, increased cell death, and increased oxidative stress, which collectively contributed to antitumor responses in vitro and in vivo." This directly confirms that V-9302 causes antitumor responses by increasing cell death and oxidative stress. The study is a preclinical development, providing strong initial evidence.

evidence.text: The intrinsic mitochondrial pathway of apoptosis is typically altered in human cancers.
evidence.source: Shifting the balance of mitochondrial apoptosis: therapeutic perspectives

evidence.text: IL-10 promotes the growth of activated CD8+ T cells in combination with low doses of IL-2.
evidence.source: Inhibitory and Stimulatory Effects of IL-10 on Human CD8+ T Cells

evidence.text: The oxidative pentose phosphate pathway (PPP) contributes to tumour growth.
evidence.source: 6-phosphogluconate dehydrogenase links oxidative PPP, lipogenesis and tumor growth by inhibiting LKB1-AMPK signaling

evidence.text: The ALK kinase inhibitor TAE684 produced greater tumor regression and improved overall survival compared with carboplatin and paclitaxel in the genetically engineered mouse model.
evidence.source: Inhibition of ALK, PI3K/MEK, and HSP90 in Murine Lung Adenocarcinoma Induced by EML4-ALK Fusion Oncogene

evidence.text: Checkpoint kinase 2 (CHEK2) is a tumour suppressor gene.
evidence.source: Can selenium be a modifier of cancer risk in CHEK2 mutation carriers?

evidence.text: BCL-2 is an antiapoptotic protein.
evidence.source: Antiapoptotic BCL-2 is required for maintenance of a model leukemia.

evidence.text: Activation-induced cell death was increased in human CD3 gamma-deficient T cell lines.
evidence.source: Contribution of CD3g to TCR regulation and signaling in human mature T lymphocytes

evidence.text: BCR/ABL-induced reactive oxygen species (ROSs) cause chronic oxidative DNA damage.
evidence.source: BCR/ABL oncogenic kinase promotes unfaithful repair of the reactive oxygen species-dependent DNA double-strand breaks.

evidence.text: V-9302 selectively and potently targets the amino acid transporter ASCT2.
evidence.source: Pharmacological Blockade of ASCT2-dependent Glutamine Transport Leads To Anti-tumor Efficacy in Preclinical Models

evidence.text: DUSP4 overexpression increased chemotherapy-induced apoptosis.
evidence.source: Profiling of residual breast cancers after neoadjuvant chemotherapy identifies DUSP4 deficiency as a mechanism of drug resistance

evidence.text: mtDNA-depleted cells had an attenuated γ-H2AX induction after treatment with mitochondrial respiratory chain function inhibitors.
evidence.source: Mitochondria-dependent signalling pathway are involved in the early process of radiation-induced bystander effects

evidence.text: Members of the Bcl-2 family of proteins are involved in the regulation of mitochondria-mediated apoptosis.
evidence.source: Shifting the balance of mitochondrial apoptosis: therapeutic perspectives

evidence.text: PDS causes checkpoint activation in HR-defective cells.
evidence.source: Targeting BRCA1 and BRCA2 Deficiencies with G-Quadruplex-Interacting Compounds

evidence.text: mTOR signalling in cancer cells regulates G-CSF.
evidence.source: Oncogenic mTOR signaling recruits myeloid-derived suppressor cells to promote tumor initiation

evidence.text: Overexpression of MUC1 in human carcinoma cells is associated with constitutive activation of NF-κB p65.
evidence.source: MUC1 oncoprotein activates the IκB kinase β complex and constitutive NF-κB signalling

evidence.text: mTORC2 is a critical regulator of amino acid metabolism in cancer.
evidence.source: mTORC2 Regulates Amino Acid Metabolism in Cancer by Phosphorylation of the Cystine-Glutamate Antiporter xCT.

evidence.text: NVP-BEZ235 inhibited the activation of Akt, S6 ribosomal protein, and 4EBP1 in breast cancer cells.
evidence.source: NVP-BEZ235, a dual PI3K/mTOR inhibitor, prevents PI3K signaling and inhibits the growth of cancer cells with activating PI3K mutations.

evidence.text: Eliminating BCL-2 yielded rapid loss of leukemic cells.
evidence.source: Antiapoptotic BCL-2 is required for maintenance of a model leukemia.

evidence.text: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy (NAC) induces a pathological complete response (pCR) in approximately 30% of patients with breast cancer.
evidence.source: Profiling of residual breast cancers after neoadjuvant chemotherapy identifies DUSP4 deficiency as a mechanism of drug resistance

evidence.text: V-9302 is a competitive small molecule antagonist of transmembrane glutamine flux.
evidence.source: Pharmacological Blockade of ASCT2-dependent Glutamine Transport Leads To Anti-tumor Efficacy in Preclinical Models
